creator: madpilot@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hi,

I first need to be sure I got one thing straight.

The error with the card appeared in gnucash even before you upgraded to 3.1, right?

You recompiled and got 3.1 as a test to try to fix the problem?

I have never used this functionality in gnucash, so I'm guessing.

My first guess is that gnucash is using some middleware software to get information from the card.

From the list of ports you gave me I'd suggest you try rebuilding all the ones that sit between gnucash and your card reader, and (still guessing) I'd try to recompile:

pcsc-lite
ccid
libchipcard
pcsc-tools
libccid


the list is probably overkill, most probably just one of these is having problems, but I have no way to know which one.

Since you're building from ports, you should also review the port options, but I don't think that's the actual problem in this case wither.

You can also try downgrading the gnucash port to 2.6.21 if that proves to be the actual problem, but from what you tell me it does not look like that is the actual cause.
